title: HTML Accessibility Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags and attributes for creating accessible HTML web pages.

questions.question: What is the primary role of web accessibility (A11y)?
options: To make websites load faster.
To enable people with disabilities to use web technologies.
To provide more colors and animations for advanced users.
To automate website development with AI.

questions.question: Which of these is the most important attribute for making images accessible to screen reader users?
options: src
alt
title
class

questions.question: How should header tags (h1-h6) be used for better accessibility?
options: Use them in any order for visual variety.
Use them to make text bigger or smaller.
Use them in a logical, nested sequence to define the document structure.
Only use h1 tags on every page.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'for' attribute in the <label> tag?
options: It defines the text shown on screen.
It links the label to its corresponding input's 'id'.
It specifies the form's action URL.
It determines the label's position.

questions.question: When should you use ARIA roles and landmarks in HTML?
options: On every single HTML element for maximum accessibility.
Only if native HTML elements aren't enough for complex widgets.
To replace standard tags like <header> and <nav>.
They are not used in modern web development.
